channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Minnesota House tie in peril after judge rules DFL lawmaker can’t be sworn in
item.description: A district court judge on Friday granted a challenge to Democratic Rep.-elect Curtis Johnson’s residency, blocking him from taking office.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans reach new plan to avoid government shutdown after back-to-back defeats
item.description: House Republicans have tentatively reached another deal to avert a partial government shutdown, as the clock ticks closer to midnight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watchdog seeks to halt 11th-hour Biden DOJ effort to ‘handcuff’ Kentucky police over Breonna Taylor incident
item.description: The Oversight Project is filing an amicus brief with a western Kentucky federal court seeking to warn against the approval of a consent decree it says will hamstring police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson says plan C reached to avert shutdown, vote expected
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Republicans have arrived at a plan C to avert a shutdown and the House will vote Friday morning on the legislation. "Yeah, yeah, we have a plan," Johnson said Friday morning as he entered the Capitol. "We're expecting votes this morning, so you all stay tuned.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Time is running short to avert a government shutdown after funding bill is rejected
item.description: A day before a potential government shutdown, the House rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s new plan Thursday to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shutdown threat looms after stop-gap spending bill fails on House floor
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump and his newest top-lieutenant, Elon Musk, have sent Washington scrambling to avoid a government shutdown, even before Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Susan Davis, Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, Elena Moore, and Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Illegal immigrant sexually abused child in the U.S. after being removed from the country five times
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials in New York arrested a Mexican-born illegal immigrant who sexually abused a child after being removed from the country five times.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance flexes his influence during spending fight
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance is flexing his influence on Capitol Hill, underscoring his role as a critical and often behind the scenes player in President-elect Trump’s orbit while other prominent figures steal the headlines.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House starts scrapping pending regulations, including student debt cancellation
item.description: President Joe Biden’s administration has started to withdraw pending regulations that won’t be finalized by the time he leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A lynching in the family inspired Michigan's first Black woman elected justice to pursue the law
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — During Michigan Supreme Court Justice Kyra Harris Bolden's first campaign, a critic told her she wasn't Michelle Obama or Kamala Harris, “but you feel emboldened to run for this office.” She later named her first child Emerson, so it could be shortened to “Em Bolden.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans scramble for funding plan C as shutdown deadline draws near
item.description: Congress is racing toward a shutdown at the end of the day Friday, and Republicans appear no closer to finding a path forward that will keep the lights on and appease President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In an early legislative test for Trump, plan B spending bill tanks in House
item.description: House Republicans failed to secure the votes needed Thursday on a spending bill amid a looming government shutdown, handing a loss to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the debt ceiling debate as a government shutdown looms in Washington
item.description: A debate over the debt ceiling is at the center of a dispute over funding that is pushing Washington to the brink of a federal government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House says Biden will visit Pope Francis, Meloni in final international trip of his presidency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden will travel to Italy next month to meet with Pope Francis and top Italian officials for what is expected to be the final international trip of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New House GOP budget proposal revives Farm Bill extension and aid money but drops ethanol provision
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — American farmers are hoping that aid to agriculture will be revived as Congress struggles to pass a short-term spending bill that would keep the federal government funded and avert a looming partial government shutdown set to begin after midnight Friday night.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans announce funding agreement, Thursday vote as shutdown looms
item.description: House Republicans have reached an agreement on a plan B to avert a government shutdown ahead of Friday’s deadline and plan to vote Thursday, lawmakers announced.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Party switch paying off? Valdes secures committee leadership after joining GOP
item.description: One state Representative in the Tampa Bay area made the controversial move to switch political parties, just last week. The news came was greeted with both cheers and criticisms.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Paxton files motion to block auction of border wall materials
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed a motion Tuesday to stop the sale of pieces of the border wall.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, NewsNation

item.title: DHS rolls out new rules for skilled foreign workers on H-1B visas
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security on Tuesday announced new rules for hiring foreign workers that was met with praise for allowing employers to fill jobs quicker and maintaining strict security oversight
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Former FBI informant will plead guilty to lying about phony bribery scheme involving the Bidens
item.description: A former FBI informant is set to plead guilty to lying about a phony bribery scheme involving President Joe Biden and his son, Hunter, that became central to a Republican impeachment inquiry in Congress.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Growing oil industry support for methane reduction rule could help it survive Trump’s return
item.description: Texas regulators are taking public comment about how they should implement an EPA rule to reduce methane leaks from the oil and gas industry.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandra Martinez, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Senator Schumer urges use of ROBIN to track mystery drones
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sen. Chuck Schumer is making a plea for federal officials to launch a declassified special drone detection system called the ROBIN – a 360-degree technology across New York, New Jersey and the metro area to track any unidentified object in the sky.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11
